title: languas korthalsii
intro: Languas korthalsii, also known as Lesser Galangal or Thai Ginger, is a type of edible rhizome plant native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the Zingiberaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which help to treat a range of health issues. The health benefits of Languas korthalsii include reducing nausea and fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and boosting the immune system. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and soups, and is also used in traditional Thai and Indonesian cooking.
